reviews.review_description:
Great Mixed Reality Game 

I keep recommending in all the forums.

Hopefully you can keep adding and fine tuning the game!

A Sci-Fi or Horror Mixed Reality game would be great if you ever develop another mixed reality project.

Keep us updated

developer_response.body:
Seriously, thank you so much for this 🙌
Seeing people recommend the game to others genuinely means a lot to us.

We’re definitely continuing to add new content and fine tune the experience, and we’ve got some exciting updates in the works already 👀

And honestly… a sci-fi or horror MR game sounds really tempting 😄
Really appreciate the support. ❤️

reviews.review_description:
Honestly, my room isn't exactly set up for mixed reality gaming. But the devs clearly thought about players like me, and that small touch made a big difference right from the start. Visually, RealityGuard is stunning. I went in expecting a pretty standard tower defense setup, but what I got felt fresh and immersive. Bringing castle defense into mixed reality is honestly a brilliant idea, and the team executed it really well. It doesn’t feel forced - it just makes the whole experience more engaging.
It’s easy to pick up, satisfying to play, and honestly just fun to watch your defenses come alive in your own space.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this ❤️
When we started building RealityGuard, one of our biggest goals was to make mixed reality feel natural and approachable no matter what kind of space players had available. We didn’t want MR to feel like a gimmick — we wanted it to feel like your own room had genuinely become part of the battlefield.

Seeing players connect with that vision really means a lot to us.

We still have a lot we want to add and improve going forward, and we’re excited to keep expanding the game with new ideas, modes, and experiences over time. Thanks again for being part of the journey 🙌

reviews.review_description: I LOVE this game. Everything is just right and the animations are smooth and it it very clear where everything is and how it works. AWESOME GAME!! (music fits aswel)

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much!! 🙌
We’re really glad you’re enjoying the game.
We’re actively working on new content (including online PvP) , and hope you’ll keep an eye on what’s coming next.
Thanks for the support! ❤️

reviews.review_description: Lose hours and use all your surroundings!

developer_response.body:
Thank you!! 🙌
We’re so glad you’re enjoying the challenge — and yes, using your surroundings is exactly the point 😄
Really appreciate you playing!

reviews.review_title: 🏰 Defend Your Lounge in MR Style!
reviews.review_description:
RealityGuard is an impressive Mixed Reality tower defense experience that turns your living room into a battlefield. Set up cannons, arrow launchers, and traps along enemy paths, then weave between your sofa, doorway, or behind a chair to stay in the action.

The MR design feels natural and immersive, especially in physical co-op, where you and a friend can defend together in real space. Strategic choices keep the game engaging — spend coins to upgrade towers or unlock new weapons, and don’t forget to wield your magical wand for extra firepower!

A solid and fun experience that combines strategy, creativity, and full-body immersion. Hours of replayable fun ahead! ⚔️

developer_response.body: Thanks for the amazing review! We’re thrilled you enjoyed the MR experience and the strategic gameplay. Can’t wait for you to try upcoming updates and new abilities!

reviews.review_title: New twist on the tower defence genre
reviews.review_description:
VR and tower defence work so well together that I've yet to really play one that I didn't like, Realty Guard is no exception.
They've done something different from the others I've played before in the genre, opting for a purely mixed reality experience and a unique take on it where your own play space is now the battlefield.
The game is in early access, but already has half the campaign and an endless mode, which I have played more than the campaign at this point.
The endless mode offers some big rewards, #1 getting 7,500 crystals that can be used to upgrade towers for more levels and added abilities, so I definitely recommend doing a run to at least get yourself on the board for the rewards.
You can upgrade a tower 5 times which unlocks a special weapon that you can use when something comes in range of said tower, Scorpion shooting a haze of poison, Cannon a barrage of explosives for example.
You don't have to sit back and let your towers do all the work, in your right hand is a sort of wizards staff where you can shoot a few abilities, currently there's three but there are more to come, ice freeze can really get you out of a bind.
The game is out the blocks in a good starting point to begin with and I'm really eager to see what it becomes further down the line.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the detailed feedback! We’re thrilled to hear you’re enjoying the MR twist on tower defense and the endless mode. Your insights really motivate us to keep improving and adding more abilities.
